response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The two most critical hard requirements — native-level Japanese language proficiency and strong C programming for embedded/UNIX systems — are both absent from his profile. His career is overwhelmingly focused on web and cloud technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript), with only peripheral C/C++ exposure from a brief 2014–2015 role at National Instruments. There is no evidence of embedded firmware development, Linux kernel-level work, or any of the required security/networking technologies such as MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, or L2/L3 switch operation.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience demonstrating strong adaptability and engineering fundamentals
Demonstrated Linux usage and some low-level systems awareness via embedded audio thesis project and C/C++ work at National Instruments
Experience with REST APIs, DevOps tooling (Docker, CI/CD), and modern backend development that touches peripheral job requirements
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency of any kind — this is a stated hard requirement that immediately disqualifies the candidate
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or networking security experience; the entire career is web, cloud, and application development
C programming background is minimal and dated; the role demands deep, current C expertise for embedded UNIX system development
